topic_title: Automation bias — trusting automated systems over human judgment

findings.location: Section 5.3 sub-heading 'Perceived Reliability and the Authority Heuristic'
findings.detail: Used as a sub-heading and explanatory label but does not appear in any prior topic's concepts_introduced. Defined inline within the same paragraph so not a hard forward-reference failure, but the sub-heading elevates it to a named concept status not sanctioned by the sequence index.

cycle1_fix_verified.location: Section 8 (Connection to AI Context), final paragraph
cycle1_fix_verified.previous_offending_text: A framework for doing this systematically — the Judgment Framework: three questions before acting on any AI output — will be introduced in Topics 9.7–9.9.
cycle1_fix_verified.current_text: A framework for doing this systematically — the Judgment Framework — will be introduced in Topics 9.7–9.9.
cycle1_fix_verified.fix_assessment: VERIFIED. The appositive clause 'three questions before acting on any AI output' has been removed. The current form is a bare name ('the Judgment Framework') followed by a topic pointer ('will be introduced in Topics 9.7–9.9'). The term is not bolded and carries no defining clause. This satisfies the mention-and-defer rule in scope_discipline.

findings.detail: The term 'authority heuristic' appears as a section sub-heading (Section 5.3) before any plain-language definition is given. The concept is explained in the paragraph body, but a beginner encounters the compound noun cold in the heading. Recommend prepending a plain-language meaning to the heading or replacing the heading with a descriptive phrase.

verdict: PASSED. Cycle 2 scope_creep fix verified: Section 8 final paragraph now reads 'the Judgment Framework — will be introduced in Topics 9.7–9.9' — bare name plus topic pointer, no appositive, not bolded. All 6 checks pass. Required sections present and non-trivial. Primary source thedecisionlab.com cited 19 times with ~82% citation density in Core Concepts; no fake citations. Word count ~3350 within the standard 3000–4400 range. No hard sequence-drift failures. One carry-over soft finding: sub-heading 'Perceived Reliability and the Authority Heuristic' in Section 5.3 uses a term not in any prior topic's concepts_introduced; surfaced to HITL #2 for awareness but does not block corpus approval.

optional_improvements.action: Rename sub-heading 'Perceived Reliability and the Authority Heuristic' to 'Perceived Reliability' or add a plain-language gloss in the first sentence of the section. The term 'authority heuristic' is not in the glossary and not in any prior topic's concepts_introduced.
